_comment: Camera placed off the (x0,x1) loop plane, sighting along x2. The 4D Klein-bottle parametrisation is S(u,v) = ((R+r cos v)cos u, (R+r cos v)sin u, r sin v cos(u/2), r sin v sin(u/2)) — i.e. its 'big loop' lives in (x0,x1) and its 'small fibre' in (x2,x3). Looking down x2 maps the big loop to a circle on screen and folds the small fibre into a strip across the third film axis, so the ring topology is visible *and* the Klein twist (u/2) is exposed. Camera is at x2 = 6 (above the loop), looking back at the origin. v_stripes=1 paints one bright/dark band per fibre so the Klein glueing is unmistakeable: traversing u from 0 → 2π brings you back to the same point on x0,x1 but with the bright/dark pattern flipped along x3.
